title: Day 65 / Fractura Total Day 18: El Jardinero ARRESTED — CJNG Commander Found Hiding in Nayarit Ditch; El Güero Conta Financial Operator Also Captured
detail: In a decisive moment that reshapes the CJNG succession crisis, CJNG's top senior regional commander and leader of the anti-O3 'Fractura Total' faction, Audias Flores Silva 'El Jardinero' (The Gardener), was captured on April 27, 2026, near the El Mirador community in Nayarit — found hiding in a roadside drainage ditch approximately 20km north of Puerto Vallarta. The 19-month precision surveillance operation deployed 500+ troops, 6 helicopters, and multiple aircraft; Flores Silva was taken into custody without shots fired as he emerged from the ditch. Mexican Security Secretary Omar García Harfuch shared aerial footage of the arrest and confirmed the operation was 'entirely executed by Mexican authorities.' The capture carries a $5 million U.S. State Department bounty. El Jardinero headed CJNG drug production and trafficking across Nayarit, Jalisco, Mexico State, and Zacatecas, and served as El Mencho's longtime security chief. PARALLEL ARREST: César Alejandro 'El Güero Conta,' El Jardinero's chief financial operator, was simultaneously arrested in Zapopan, Jalisco on charges of money laundering and asset acquisition. CJNG RETALIATION — NAYARIT: Cartel gunmen immediately deployed roadblocks on Federal Highway 15 at Ahuacatlán, Copales, Ixtlán del Río, and Jala, and on the Tepic-Mazatlán highway at Tecuala and Acaponeta. Six vehicles and six businesses were burned; no deaths or injuries reported. All roadblocks cleared and highways, schools, and businesses returned to normal by morning of April 28. U.S. Ambassador Ronald Johnson publicly praised Mexican SEMAR and the security cabinet. SIGNIFICANCE FOR FRACTURA TOTAL: El Jardinero's arrest effectively ends the declared anti-O3 armed opposition. With his faction's leader captured and financial operator detained, O3/Los Cuinis emerges as the unchallenged dominant CJNG leadership faction. El Sapo (Hugo Gonzalo Mendoza Gaytán) remains the last standing senior leader of the anti-O3 faction. Mexico has now neutralized four of CJNG's top commanders below El Mencho within 65 days: El Tuli (killed Feb 22), El Doble R (arrested March 11), El Jardinero (arrested April 27), El Güero Conta (arrested April 27).

media.caption: Audias Flores Silva 'El Jardinero,' top CJNG commander, captured April 27, 2026 — found hiding in a roadside ditch near El Mirador, Nayarit, 20km north of Puerto Vallarta

title: CIA Sovereignty Crisis: Chihuahua Governor Maru Campos Declines Senate Hearing — 'Not Mandatory'; Senate Condemns 'Institutional Crisis'
detail: Chihuahua Governor María Eugenia Campos Galván 'Maru Campos' did not attend the Mexican Senate hearing called for April 28, 2026 at 11:00 hours before the Constitutional Points and Public Security commissions, which had been formally convened to address CIA officers' participation in counter-narcotics raids without federal authorization. Maru Campos sent an official letter declining to appear, stating her absence was 'to not compromise ongoing investigations.' Her direct statement: 'No, we are not going to attend the Senate today because it is not mandatory. We answer to our people, not to the Senate.' Senate Political Coordination Board president Ignacio Mier condemned the no-show as an 'institutional crisis in Chihuahua' and Morena senators moved to escalate the confrontation. President Sheinbaum's reaction: 'The investigation must continue,' but declined to impose specific sanctions for non-attendance. BILATERAL CONTEXT: The hearing concerned the April 19-20 CIA vehicle crash in Chihuahua's Sierra Tarahumara that killed two CIA officers (Richard Leiter Johnston III, 36, and John Dudley Black, 44) along with Chihuahua State Investigation Agency chief Pedro Román Oseguera Cervantes and bodyguard Manuel Genaro Méndez Montes, during the CIA's third counter-narcotics operation against Sinaloa Cartel drug labs in Chihuahua this year. SIGNIFICANCE FOR CJNG TRACKER: The Senate's failure to obtain Maru Campos' testimony represents a constitutional deadlock on U.S. intelligence operations on Mexican soil — the same operational framework that enabled the Tapalpa operation killing El Mencho. Sheinbaum's April 24-25 governance reform letter to all 32 governors stands as executive policy but without legislative codification from the failed Senate hearing.

media.caption: La Silla Rota: Maru Campos will not attend the Senate — 'Investigation must continue,' says Sheinbaum after governor declines CIA agents hearing
